body.$value: Why does Google recommend that an organization maintain more than one super administrator account?
So a lost or compromised super-admin account can be reset by another super admin
To share the workload of answering daily staff email
Because each Google Workspace license requires its own administrator
To increase the number of recovery phone numbers Google keeps on file
True or False: A break-glass emergency admin account should be used for daily administrative work so it stays active and familiar.
True
False
Your district has lost access to its domain registrar and DNS zone. Which super-admin recovery path is now blocked?
The domain-ownership (TXT/CNAME) verification escalation with Google support
A second super admin resetting the locked admin's password
An admin generating backup verification codes for a user
Reactivating a suspended user account
What is the recommended way to store a break-glass account's backup verification codes?
Printed and stored offline in a secure physical location
In a Google Doc shared with all super admins
Emailed to the IT distribution list for quick access
Saved in the browser password manager on the admin's laptop
True or False: On Google Workspace for Education editions, super-admin self-recovery is OFF by default, so you must maintain another recovery path such as a second super admin.
True
False
